attributes.action: Reinstating the Death Penalty for Child Sex Abusers & Rapists
attributes.additional_details: The perpetration of such acts against children is viewed by many as the most egregious offence imaginable. By taking decisive action and making it clear that this behaviour will no longer be tolerated, society sends a strong message that the protection of children is paramount. Implementing the most severe consequences is seen not only as a reflection of society’s moral stance but also as a potential source of hope for victims. The assurance that those who commit these crimes will be met with the utmost seriousness may offer some comfort to children and their families, reinforcing the commitment to safeguarding their wellbeing.
attributes.background: To reinstate the death penalty specifically for those convicted of child sexual abuse. The reasoning behind this demand is rooted in the belief that child sexual abuse represents the most heinous crime imaginable, inflicting profound and lasting suffering on its victims.

rejection.details:
You may wish to sign the following petition, which calls for similar action:

Introduce the death penalty for certain child sex offences
https://petition.parliament.uk/petitions/762088
